decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field along with other cards to generate a large amount of mana quickly in order to play powerful spells and overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes cards like Arboreal Grazer and Crumbling Vestige to help ramp up mana, while cards like Scapeshift and Formidable Speaker provide additional benefits from having multiple lands in play. The goal is to quickly assemble the necessary components to execute a game-winning combo and outpace the opponent before they can develop their own strategy.

This deck focuses on aggressive plays with a combination of efficient creatures and disruptive spells to control the game. The deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early on with creatures like Gifted Aetherborn and Bonecrusher Giant while using cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Additionally, the deck includes powerful finishers like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to close out games quickly. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive strategy with a mix of dual lands and utility lands to ensure consistent gameplay.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. By utilizing mana dorks like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic, the deck aims to ramp quickly into powerful creatures like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben disrupts the opponent's game plan while cards like Enduring Innocence and Collected Company provide additional value. The deck also includes support cards like The Wandering Emperor and Elspeth, Storm Slayer to close out games. Overall, this deck focuses on efficient creatures and disruptive elements to maintain pressure and tempo.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, as well as hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress. The creatures in the deck provide additional value and can help stabilize the board. The mana base is designed to support the various black and green spells in the deck, while also including utility lands like Mutavault and Boseiju, Who Endures. The goal is to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while slowly building up resources and eventually overwhelming them with powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse.

The deck focuses on utilizing a diverse range of legendary creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. By combining powerful creatures like Jhoira, Rona, and Cecil with support cards like Mox Amber and Relic of Legends, the deck aims to establish board dominance and maintain pressure throughout the game. The inclusion of a variety of colors allows for flexibility in casting spells and provides access to a wide range of abilities, making it a formidable force in 1 vs 1 matches.

The main focus of this Mono Black Control deck is to utilize a combination of efficient removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful creatures to disrupt the opponent's game plan and gain control of the game. The deck aims to outvalue opponents by generating card advantage through Thoughtseize and Duress while applying pressure with creatures like Gifted Aetherborn and Archfiend of the Dross. Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Blade of the Oni provide additional threats that can take over the game if left unchecked. The deck's strong mana base allows for consistent casting of spells and utilizing Mutavault for additional threats.

The key concept of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through a combination of hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize, removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, and controlling creatures like Sheoldred and Sentinel of the Nameless City. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by constantly removing threats and generating incremental advantage with cards like Overlord of the Balemurk and Culling Ritual. The mana base is designed to support the deck's strategy of controlling the game and outlasting the opponent in a grindy matchup.

The primary focus of this deck is to use the Greasefang creature to combo with Parhelion II, creating a powerful synergy that can quickly overwhelm opponents. The deck includes a variety of support cards such as Raffine's Informant, Formidable Speaker, and Cache Grab to help set up the combo and protect key pieces. Additionally, the deck includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Collective Brutality to control the game and ensure a smoother execution of the combo. Overall, the deck aims to establish board presence and generate incremental advantages to secure victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing the combo between Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful board presence. Fleeting Spirit and Guardian of New Benalia can provide additional support, while Monument to Endurance can help protect key pieces. Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse offer removal options, while Thoughseize can disrupt opponents' strategies. The deck also includes some land manipulation with cards like Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth and Godless Shrine. Overall, the goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with strong creatures and synergies.

This deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field to generate a large amount of mana and cast powerful spells quickly. It includes cards like Vizier of Tumbling Sands and Hidden Strings to untap Lotus Field, enabling explosive turns. The deck also includes cards like Ultimatum','','');">Emergent Ultimatum and Peer into the Abyss to search for key combo pieces and finish the game. By assembling the right combination of cards and efficiently generating mana, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with powerful spells and win the game in a single turn.

This deck focuses on utilizing the card advantage and selection provided by Niv-Mizzet Reborn to control the game. With a variety of powerful multicolored cards and versatile removal options, the deck aims to outvalue opponents while efficiently answering threats. The inclusion of Bring to Light allows for flexible tutoring, ensuring access to key cards when needed. The mana base is designed to support the diverse color requirements of the deck, with the potential to ramp and fix mana thanks to cards like Sylvan Caryatid and Leyline Binding.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy, utilizing low-cost creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear to deal quick damage. The player can also utilize spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to control the board and remove threats. The deck aims to maintain pressure through the use of powerful creatures like Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Emberheart Challenger, with additional support from cards like Monstrous Rage and Screaming Nemesis. Overall, the deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently to secure a victory.

The deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with fast and aggressive creatures like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Kumano Faces Kakkazan. It also includes burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to deal direct damage to the opponent or remove blockers. The deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total and win the game before they have a chance to stabilize. The inclusion of lands like Ramunap Ruins and Den of the Bugbear provides additional reach and damage potential, while cards like Monstrous Rage and Emberheart Challenger enhance the aggression of the deck.

The main idea of this deck is to use early mana accelerators like Llanowar Elves to ramp into powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Sentinel of the Nameless City while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. The deck aims to control the board with removal spells like Abrupt Decay and Tear Asunder, while generating card advantage with Unholy Annex / Ritual Chamber. The deck also includes versatile creatures like Badgermole Cub and Professor Dellian Fel to further disrupt the opponent's strategy and secure victory.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly establish board presence and apply pressure on opponents. The deck includes efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter, along with spell cards like Fiery Impulse and Academic Dispute to deal damage and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes card draw spells like Consider and Sleight of Hand to maintain card advantage. Overall, the deck aims to win the game by aggressively attacking the opponent while also being able to control the board with its spell cards.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly deploy low-cost creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, supported by burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to control the board and deal direct damage to the opponent. The deck also includes card draw and card selection to maintain momentum and close out games efficiently. With a focus on speed and aggression, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize, utilizing a mix of creatures, burn spells, and card advantage to secure victory.
